title: Add CLI Skeleton And Specs Initialization
description: Create the standalone `spec.py` entrypoint with shebang, argument parser skeleton, shared specs-path constants, top-level clean error handling, and the strict `init` subcommand behavior.
acceptance_criteria: `skills/spec-management/spec.py` exists with `#!/usr/bin/env python3` and is intended to be executable.
`spec init` creates `specs/`, `specs/drafts/`, `specs/planned/`, and `specs/done/` in an empty project root.
`spec init` exits with the specified error when `specs/` already exists, without modifying the tree.
All non-`init` commands are wired to fail with the explicit `specs/ not found` remediation message when the specs tree is absent.
Unhandled exceptions are caught at the top level and reported as clean stderr errors without tracebacks.

handoff_summary.completed:
- Created `skills/spec-management/spec.py` with `#!/usr/bin/env python3` shebang and executable permissions
- Implemented `spec init`: creates specs/{drafts,planned,done}/, exits 1 with explicit message if specs/ already exists
- All non-init subcommands call `_require_specs_dir()` first — exits 1 with remediation message if specs/ absent
- Top-level `try/except` in `__main__` block catches all unhandled exceptions and prints clean `error: <msg>` to stderr, re-raising SystemExit to preserve normal exit-code flow
- Argument parser skeleton wires all six subcommands (init, create, list, show, set, migrate) with full argument definitions
handoff_summary.remaining:
- `cmd_create` — create spec in drafts/ with frontmatter (future bead)
- `cmd_list` — list specs across all three folders with filter support (future bead)
- `cmd_show` — show frontmatter + first section (future bead)
- `cmd_set` — update frontmatter fields and move files (future bead)
- `cmd_migrate` — add frontmatter to legacy specs (future bead)
- `skills/spec-management/SKILL.md` update to reference spec.py (future bead)
- `skills/spec-management/tests/test_spec.py` unit tests (future tester bead)
handoff_summary.risks: None. This bead makes no changes to `src/` — zero risk to existing orchestrator functionality. Stub subcommands raise NotImplementedError which is caught by the top-level handler and printed as a clean error.
handoff_summary.next_action: Implement create, list, show, set, migrate subcommands in subsequent beads as planned in the feature tree.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts. Only new file created in skills/spec-management/; no src/ files touched.

conflict_risks: No conflicts. Only new file created in skills/spec-management/; no src/ files touched.

execution_history.summary: Created `skills/spec-management/spec.py` — a standalone CLI skeleton with shebang, executable bit, shared path constants (`SPECS_DIR`, `DRAFTS_DIR`, `PLANNED_DIR`, `DONE_DIR`), a `_require_specs_dir()` guard used by every non-`init` subcommand, the fully implemented `cmd_init` (creates all three subdirs, exits 1 if `specs/` already exists), parser stubs for `create`/`list`/`show`/`set`/`migrate`, and a top-level exception handler that converts unhandled exceptions to clean stderr messages.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
